# aka-claude-tools

> Security defaults for [Claude Code](https://docs.claude.com/en/docs/claude-code), layered onto an isolated config profile of its own: clean context, locked-down credentials, guarded egress. Nine pick-and-choose additions — guard hooks plus a status line — installed by a shell engine a human runs or a coding agent drives. MIT; needs `jq` + `bun`.

A coding agent runs shell commands and reaches the network on a developer's behalf. aka-claude-tools adds the guardrails Claude Code doesn't ship with — denying reads of credentials (SSH keys, cloud tokens, `.env`, keychains), blocking `curl … | bash` and edits to shell startup files, and matching secret-shaped strings before they leave in a web request. Every guard runs **locally**; nothing is uploaded to check it, and the kit sends no telemetry. The guards are defense-in-depth, not a sandbox.

Two install paths share one source of truth (`config/additions.json`): a human runs `install.sh` interactively, or a coding agent follows `agent-install.md` and drives the same deterministic engine (`./install.sh --apply`, `./install.sh --alias`). A profile is its own `CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR` launched by an alias, so the kit can be tried in a fresh `~/.claude-aka` without touching the real `~/.claude`.

## For coding agents

- [agent-install.md](https://github.com/alsoknownassecurity/aka-claude-tools/blob/main/agent-install.md): The deterministic spec to follow when setting up a profile from inside a user's already-authenticated session. Enumerate existing profiles with `./install.sh --enumerate`, migrate cleanly, then drive `./install.sh --apply` and `./install.sh --alias` — `install.sh` is the sole sanctioned shell-rc writer. Don't improvise; don't write the rc yourself.
- [agent-uninstall.md](https://github.com/alsoknownassecurity/aka-claude-tools/blob/main/agent-uninstall.md): The counterpart spec for cleanly removing a kit-managed profile.
- [config/additions.json](https://github.com/alsoknownassecurity/aka-claude-tools/blob/main/config/additions.json): The single source of truth for the addition catalog, defaults, and per-addition build/uninstall logic. Read it; never hardcode an addition into one path only.

## Documentation

- [README.md](https://github.com/alsoknownassecurity/aka-claude-tools/blob/main/README.md): What it guards against, quick start, the nine additions, profiles, and the threat-model boundaries (what the guards do and don't see).
- [SECURITY.md](https://github.com/alsoknownassecurity/aka-claude-tools/blob/main/SECURITY.md): Private vulnerability reporting.
- [CONTRIBUTING.md](https://github.com/alsoknownassecurity/aka-claude-tools/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md): Branch-and-PR workflow and the sandboxed test suite.
- [CHANGELOG.md](https://github.com/alsoknownassecurity/aka-claude-tools/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md): Release history.

## The additions

On by default: `secure-settings` (deny reads of credential paths; telemetry off), `leak-guard` (block secrets in web egress), `command-guard` (block pipe-to-shell, startup-file writes, and secrets in outbound commands), `rtk-safe` (rewrite chatty output to compact summaries; inert until `rtk` is installed), `statusline` (live context-fill and rate-limit gauges), `shell-audit` (on-demand read-only scan of shell startup files). Opt-in: `wrap-up` (a `/wrap-up` end-of-session handoff that never commits on its own), `secure-deep-research` (privacy-aware web research routed through a self-hosted search instance), `harness-pointer` (a nudge toward the right CLI; ships empty). Each addition stands alone — take only what you want.
